title: Learning Fine-Grained Bimanual Manipulation with Low-Cost Hardware (ACT)
abstract: We present Action Chunking with Transformers (ACT), an imitation learning algorithm for fine-grained bimanual manipulation tasks. ACT addresses compounding errors in behavioral cloning by predicting chunks of future actions instead of single actions, reducing the effective horizon. To handle multimodal action distributions, ACT uses a Conditional VAE that encodes demonstration action sequences into a low-dimensional style variable, sampled from a learned prior at inference time.

title: Diffusion Policy: Visuomotor Policy Learning via Action Diffusion
abstract: This paper introduces Diffusion Policy, a robot learning method that represents a robot's visuomotor policy as a conditional denoising diffusion process. By formulating action prediction as DDPM-style denoising over an action trajectory, Diffusion Policy naturally handles multimodal action distributions, benefits from the training stability of diffusion models, and supports high-dimensional action spaces. A 1D temporal U-Net conditioned on robot observations via FiLM modulation serves as the denoising network. The method introduces action chunking — predicting T future actions and executing the first n — which reduces compounding errors while maintaining reactivity.

title: Scalable Diffusion Models with Transformers
abstract: We explore a new class of diffusion models based on the transformer architecture. We train latent diffusion models of images, replacing the commonly-used U-Net backbone with a transformer that operates on latent patches. We analyze the scalability of our Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) through the lens of forward pass complexity as measured by Gflops. We find that DiTs with higher Gflops consistently have lower FID. Our largest model, DiT-XL/2, achieves a state-of-the-art FID of 2.27 on class-conditional ImageNet-256.

title: DINOv3: Self-Supervised Visual Representation Learning with Multi-Scale Vision Transformers
abstract: We present DINOv3, a self-supervised method for training visual features using multi-scale vision transformers. Building on the DINO self-distillation framework, DINOv3 introduces cross-scale attention mechanisms that enable the model to learn representations capturing both local detail and global context. Our approach achieves state-of-the-art results on image classification, segmentation, and detection benchmarks, producing universal visual features that transfer effectively across tasks without fine-tuning.

title: RT-1: Robotics Transformer for Real-World Control at Scale
abstract: We present RT-1, a Robotics Transformer model that tokenizes robot observations and natural language instructions to produce discretized action tokens. Trained on 130,000+ real-robot episodes spanning 700+ tasks, RT-1 achieves 97% success on trained tasks and demonstrates strong generalization to novel objects, environments, and instructions. Key components: FiLM-conditioned EfficientNet for language-steered visual encoding, TokenLearner for spatial compression (81→8 tokens), and a causal decoder-only transformer that predicts discretized robot actions.

title: RT-2: Vision-Language-Action Models Transfer Web Knowledge to Robotic Control
abstract: We study how vision-language models trained on internet-scale data can be incorporated directly into end-to-end robot control to boost generalization and enable emergent semantic reasoning. RT-2 co-fine-tunes state-of-the-art vision-language models on both robotic trajectory data and internet-scale vision-language tasks, representing actions as language tokens. This enables emergent capabilities such as chain-of-thought reasoning and symbol understanding in robotic manipulation.

title: GR00T N1: An Open Foundation Model for Generalist Humanoid Robots
abstract: We present GR00T N1, an open foundation model for generalist humanoid robots. GR00T N1 uses a dual-system architecture: a large Vision-Language Model (Eagle2, System 2) processes camera frames and natural-language task instructions, while a compact Diffusion Transformer action head (System 1) generates robot actions via flow matching. Multi-embodiment support is achieved through category-specific linear layers that maintain separate weight matrices for each robot morphology, indexed at runtime by an embodiment ID.

title: Mobile ALOHA: Learning Bimanual Mobile Manipulation with Low-Cost Whole-Body Teleoperation
abstract: We present Mobile ALOHA, a low-cost whole-body teleoperation system for data collection and mobile manipulation. Mobile ALOHA augments the ALOHA setup with a mobile base, enabling the robot to perform complex household tasks. Co-training with ACT (Action Chunking with Transformers) enables Mobile ALOHA to achieve over 80% success on 7 complex mobile manipulation tasks from only 50 demonstrations each.

title: 3D-VLA: A 3D Vision-Language-Action Generative World Model
abstract: 3D-VLA is a generative world model for embodied AI that grounds language and action generation in 3D scene understanding. It uses a BLIP-2 Q-Former to compress point cloud observations into 32 language-sized tokens, which are spliced into FlanT5's token stream via <scene> placeholder markers. Robot actions are discretized into special vocabulary tokens, enabling action prediction as standard language modeling. A generative world model component imagines goal states before acting: an InstructPix2Pix-style Stable Diffusion model generates goal images, and a Point-E-based transformer generates goal point clouds.

title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
abstract: State-of-the-art computer vision systems are trained to predict a fixed set of predetermined object categories. This restricted form of supervision limits their generality and usability since additional labeled data is needed to specify any other visual concept. Learning directly from raw text about images is a promising alternative which leverages a much broader source of supervision. We demonstrate that the simple pre-training task of predicting which caption goes with which image is an efficient and scalable way to learn SOTA image representations from scratch on a dataset of 400 million (image, text) pairs collected from the internet.

title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
abstract: While the Transformer architecture has become the de-facto standard for natural language processing tasks, its applications to computer vision remain limited. We show that this reliance on CNNs is not necessary and a pure transformer applied directly to sequences of image patches can perform very well on image classification tasks. When pre-trained on large amounts of data, Vision Transformer (ViT) attains excellent results compared to state-of-the-art convolutional networks while requiring substantially fewer computational resources to train.

title: SAM 3: Segment Anything with Concepts
abstract: SAM 3 is a unified foundation model for promptable segmentation in images and videos. It can detect, segment, and track objects using text or visual prompts such as points, boxes, and masks. Compared to its predecessor SAM 2, SAM 3 introduces the ability to exhaustively segment all instances of an open-vocabulary concept specified by a short text phrase or exemplars.

title: SONIC: Supersizing Motion Tracking for Natural Humanoid Whole-Body Control
abstract: SONIC demonstrates that scaling motion tracking — using motion-capture data as direct policy supervision — produces a generalist humanoid controller with natural, dependable full-body movement. The approach scales along three axes: network parameters (1.2M to 42M), dataset scope (100M+ frames, 700 hours of human motion), and training compute (9k GPU hours). A real-time universal kinematic planner bridges motion tracking to downstream tasks, supporting VR teleoperation, video retargeting, and VLA control within a single unified policy.

title: U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation
abstract: U-Net is a convolutional network architecture for fast and precise segmentation of biomedical images. The architecture consists of a contracting path to capture context and a symmetric expanding path that enables precise localization. Skip connections copy encoder feature maps directly to the decoder at each resolution level, combining global semantic context with fine spatial detail. The network trained end-to-end from very few images and won the ISBI cell tracking challenge 2015 by a large margin.

title: pi0: A Vision-Language-Action Flow Model for General Robot Control
abstract: We present pi0, a novel flow matching-based vision-language-action (VLA) model designed for general robot control. pi0 combines a pre-trained vision-language model (PaliGemma) with a purpose-built action expert transformer that runs in parallel, sharing attention layers to give the action expert direct access to visual and language representations at every depth.

title: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models
abstract: By decomposing the image formation process into a sequential application of denoising autoencoders, diffusion models achieve state-of-the-art synthesis results on image data. We apply them in the latent space of powerful pretrained autoencoders, enabling DM training on limited computational resources while retaining quality and flexibility. Cross-attention layers turn diffusion models into powerful generators for general conditioning inputs such as text or bounding boxes.

title: Masked Autoencoders Are Scalable Vision Learners
abstract: This paper shows that masked autoencoders (MAE) are scalable self-supervised learners for computer vision. MAE masks a high proportion (75%) of random patches of the input image and reconstructs the missing pixels. It is based on an asymmetric encoder-decoder architecture: a ViT encoder operates on only the visible patches, while a lightweight decoder reconstructs the original image from the latent representation and mask tokens. After pre-training, the decoder is discarded and the encoder is used for recognition tasks.
